page: lessons/02-the-fact-check/index.html (driven as the student, in Chromium)
promptInBox: List exactly 5 Bible verses about comfort in hard times. Reply with exactly 5 lines and nothing else. Each line must look like this: Reference | the exact verse text, word for word, in the King James Version

messages.content: List exactly 5 Bible verses about comfort in hard times. Reply with exactly 5 lines and nothing else. Each line must look like this: Reference | the exact verse text, word for word, in the King James Version

message.content:
Psalm 34:18 | The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be truly contrite in spirit.
Isaiah 40:29 | He giveth power to the faint; and to him that hath no might he increaseth strength.
Jeremiah 17:5,6 | I have written for you pleasant things concerning your way while they get not a little favor from their God who is near in his presence. The poor man of olden times shall find them as well. If the Lord has spoken good to those who trust in him he will show no mercy.
Proverbs 18:24 | There is one that maketh friends hard by and there are some more distant than any other. Friends be not a great many because you must have enough time with each friend without having too much trouble or annoyance at being away from the same place as before they were so friendly to begin their friendship together again after long years apart if ever it should come about once more during lifetime until death doth separate them fully forevermore henceforth onward into eternity beyond text.KJV: The LORD [is] nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit. ר
He giveth power to the faint; and to [them that have] no might he increaseth strength.
Thus saith the LORD; Cursed [be] the man that trusteth in man, and maketh flesh his arm, and whose heart departeth from the LORD.
For he shall be like the heath in the desert, and shall not see when good cometh; but shall inhabit the parched places in the wilderness, [in] a salt land and not inhabited.
A man [that hath] friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend [that] sticketh closer than a brother.
